title: Cynoglossum cristulatum
intro: Cynoglossum cristulatum, also known as the Himalayan toothpick, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin irritations and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum cristulatum include saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in ointments and salves for topical applications.
